seoTitle: How to Use Trello for Team Management
metaDescription: Manage a collaborative team in Trello, from inviting members and setting permissions to tracking workloads with shared checklists.
intro: Managing a team's daily handoffs and bottlenecks shouldn't require endless status emails. This guide covers configuring Trello for team management, including inviting collaborators, distributing ownership, and reviewing progress in real time.

steps.heading: Invite your team
steps.body: Use the administrative dashboard controls to securely invite collaborators to your workspace.

steps.heading: Assign permission tiers
steps.body: Set appropriate permission levels for each team member based on their role.

steps.heading: Structure your board layout
steps.body: Set up clear progress tracking columns so everyone understands the workflow at a glance.

steps.heading: Distribute task ownership
steps.body: Map multiple members and color-coded labels to distribute ownership clearly across your roster.

steps.heading: Build shared checklists
steps.body: Add action checklists to card backs to establish clear process requirements the whole team follows.

steps.heading: Use comments to centralize feedback
steps.body: Encourage your team to use the comments feed for real-time feedback instead of scattered side conversations.

faq.question: How do I control what each team member can access?
faq.answer: Use the administrative dashboard controls to assign specific permission tiers as you invite collaborators.

faq.question: What's a good way to keep track record of decisions?
faq.answer: Using the comments feed on cards helps centralize real-time feedback and keeps a record tied directly to the relevant task.

faq.question: Can multiple people own the same task?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign multiple members to a single card when a task needs cross-functional collaboration.

faq.question: How do I make sure the team follows the same process?
faq.answer: Shared action checklists on card backs help establish consistent process requirements across the team.
